MAYOR Will TRY


TO PLACE BLAME

pages.raw_text:
blame should be placed significant
ly point to the ordinance which
ordered the board Of public works
to allow the Washington Water
Power company to repair the
bridge.


At that time the city engineers
office refused absolutely to have
anything to DO with the matter
when the council turned down the
proposition to construct a new steel
bridge.


For several years past close
study of what has caused the col
lapse of bridges all over the coun-
try has shown that it has always
been reconstruction and the use
of the old timbering and steel.


Knowing this. the city council
ordained that the Washington
Water Power company should con.
struct the bridge.


To Make Mccormick Scapegoat.


Efforts are being made to Shout
der the blame upon ]. ]. McCor-
mick, inspector Of bridges, but it
Is said that to all appearances the
bridge was safe and the fault lies
directly with the Washington
Water Power company for not dis
covering the decayed timbers when
testing the bridge preparatory to
